key_insight: H-Res adapts frozen Transformer memories by steering activations on the manifold, shaping the residual energy landscape instead of changing weights or adding prompt tokens.
engineering_relevance: This offers a low-overhead, stability-preserving adaptation strategy for large associative models, useful for scalable deployment in structured scientific and engineering domains.
method: Learns a state-dependent vector field that controls token trajectories in activation space.
Modulates the effective energy landscape while keeping the base model’s global equilibrium intact.
Avoids sequence-length growth and direct synaptic modification.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 26% better performance than global weight modification on associative retrieval tasks and scalable results on structured-domain benchmarks.
watch_item: The method may require careful control-design and validation to ensure the learned steering field remains stable and does not unintentionally distort retrieval behavior.
paper_title: Parallel Manifold Steering: Efficient Adaptation of Large Associative Memories via Residual Energy Shaping
